title: काली स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री काली स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ कालीं कंकालमालिनीं महाकालसमीपगाम्।
मुण्डमालाधरां देवीं महाकालीं नमाम्यहम्॥१॥

खड्गं कपालं डमरुं त्रिशूलं धारयन्तीं करचतुष्टयेन।
मुण्डस्रजं रक्तविलोलनेत्रां महाकालीं प्रणमामि शश्वत्॥२॥

घोररूपां महारावां सर्वशत्रुक्षयंकरीम्।
भक्तानुग्रहदां देवीं काली प्रणमाम्यहम्॥३॥

त्वं विद्या सर्वलोकानां त्वं माया विश्वमोहिनी।
त्वं श्रीस्त्वमीश्वरी देवी त्वमहंकार एव च॥४॥

कालरात्रिर्महारात्रिर्मोहरात्रिश्च दारुणा।
त्वं श्रीस्त्वं च महाविद्या महामाया तथैव च॥५॥

नमो देव्यै महादेव्यै दुर्गायै सततं नमः।
नमो भगवती काली करालवदने नमः॥६॥

नमामि त्वां महाकालि जगद्धात्रि हरप्रिये।
महाभयविनाशिनि महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥७॥

महामारी महाचण्डे महासुरविनाशिनि।
श्मशानवासिनी घोरे महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥८॥

रक्तबीजवधे देवि रक्तपानप्रिये सदा।
रक्ताम्बरधरे चण्डि महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥९॥

शववाहनसंस्थे च शवाभरणभूषिते।
महाप्रलयकारिण्यै महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१०॥

दंष्ट्राकरालवदने लोलजिह्वे भयंकरि।
कराले विकटे घोरे महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥११॥

चामुण्डे मुण्डमथिनि चण्डमुण्डविनाशिनि।
कालि कंकालि कामाक्षि महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१२॥

शूलिनी कपालिनी घोररूपे भैरवि भीषणे।
कालि काली महाकालि सिद्धिदे सिद्धिदायिनि॥१३॥

इदं स्तोत्रं महेशानि काल्याः परमदुर्लभम्।
त्रिसन्ध्यं यः पठेन्नित्यं तस्य लक्ष्मीः स्थिरा भवेत्॥१४॥

॥ इति श्री काली स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Kali Stotram ||

Om Kalim Kankala-malinim Mahakala-samipagam.
Munda-mala-dharam Devim Mahakalim Namamyaham.||1||

Khadgam Kapalam Damarum Trishulam Dharayantim Kara-chatushtayena.
Munda-srajam Rakta-vilola-netram Mahakalim Pranamami Shashvat.||2||

Ghora-rupam Maharavam Sarva-shatru-kshayamkarim.
Bhakta-anugraha-dam Devim Kali Pranamamyaham.||3||

Tvam Vidya Sarva-lokanam Tvam Maya Vishva-mohini.
Tvam Shris Tvam Ishvari Devi Tvam Ahankara Eva Cha.||4||

Kalaratrir Maharatrir Moharatris Cha Daruna.
Tvam Shris Tvam Cha Mahavidya Mahamaya Tathaiva Cha.||5||

Namo Devyai Mahadevyai Durgayai Satatam Namah.
Namo Bhagavati Kali Karala-vadane Namah.||6||

Namami Tvam Mahakali Jagaddhatri Hara-priye.
Maha-bhaya-vinashini Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||7||

Mahamari Mahachande Mahasura-vinashini.
Shmashana-vasini Ghore Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||8||

Raktabija-vadhe Devi Rakta-pana-priye Sada.
Raktambara-dhare Chandi Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||9||

Shava-vahana-samsthe Cha Shava-bharana-bhushite.
Maha-pralaya-karinyai Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||10||

Danshtra-karala-vadane Lola-jihve Bhayankari.
Karale Vikate Ghore Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||11||

Chamunde Munda-mathini Chanda-munda-vinashini.
Kali Kankali Kamakshi Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||12||

Shulini Kapalini Ghora-rupe Bhairavi Bhishane.
Kali Kali Mahakali Siddhide Siddhi-dayini.||13||

Idam Stotram Maheshani Kalyah Parama-durlabham.
Tri-sandhyam Yah Pathen-nityam Tasya Lakshmih Sthira Bhavet.||14||

|| Iti Shri Kali Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
कंकाल की माला धारण करने वाली, महाकाल के समीप रहने वाली, मुण्डमाला धारिणी महाकाली देवी को मैं नमस्कार करता हूँ।

चार हाथों में खड्ग, कपाल, डमरु और त्रिशूल धारण करने वाली, मुण्डों की माला पहनने वाली, रक्त-लोल नेत्रों वाली महाकाली को सदा प्रणाम।

आप सब लोकों की विद्या हैं, विश्वमोहिनी माया हैं। आप श्री हैं, ईश्वरी हैं। आप कालरात्रि, महारात्रि और मोहरात्रि हैं।

हे जगद्धात्री, हरप्रिये! महाभय का विनाश करने वाली महाकाली को नमस्कार। हे श्मशानवासिनी! महासुरों का विनाश करने वाली को नमस्कार।

रक्तबीज का वध करने वाली, शववाहन पर स्थित, महाप्रलय करने वाली, विकराल दंष्ट्रा वाली, चामुण्डा, चण्डमुण्ड विनाशिनी महाकाली को नमस्कार।

जो इस स्तोत्र को त्रिसन्ध्या नित्य पढ़ता है, उसकी लक्ष्मी स्थिर होती है।
benefits: Invokes the fierce protective power of Goddess Mahakali
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies, evil forces, and negative energies
Traditionally recited for removal of deep-rooted fears and grants fearlessness
Invokes stable prosperity (sthira lakshmi) when recited thrice daily
Provides protection from black magic and malefic influences
Invokes the blessing of spiritual power and tantric siddhis
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Kali, Kali Stotram (काली स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Rooted in Tantra Shastra, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to grant stable prosperity (sthira Lakshmi) when recited thrice daily and provide protection from black magic and malefic influences. It is also said to invoke the fierce protective power of Goddess Mahakali. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Navratri, Kali Puja, and Diwali. With 14 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Kali Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Kali Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful prayer to Goddess Mahakali for protection, fearlessness, and destruction of evil forces.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Kali Stotram and what is its source?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kali Stotram is a fourteen-verse Sanskrit hymn dedicated to Goddess Kali, opening with Om Kalim kankala-malinim mahakala-samipagam. It is preserved in the Tantra Shastra tradition and appears in several Shakta tantric anthologies and stotra manuals. The hymn praises Kali through her classical iconography (skull garland, four arms holding sword-skull-damaru-trishula, bloody tongue, naked digambari form) and identifies her with the supreme reality (parama vidya), the cosmic illusion power (mahamaya), the dissolver of time (kalaratri), and Shiva's consort. It concludes with the assurance of stable prosperity (sthira lakshmi) for those who recite it three times daily. (Sources: Tantra Shastra anthologies; Karpuradi Stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Kali Stotram different from the Mahakali Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The two texts overlap heavily in iconography and devotional intent but differ in length, source, and emphasis. This Kali Stotram is a fourteen-verse hymn from the general Tantra Shastra tradition focusing on the universal Kali as Mahamaya, Kalaratri and Sthira Lakshmi. The Mahakali Stotram, by contrast, is a fifteen-verse hymn specifically attributed to the Brahmavaivarta Purana, spoken by Brahma at the time of the Madhu-Kaitabha episode when he invoked Yoganidra Mahakali to awaken Vishnu. The Mahakali Stotram emphasises the ten-armed first-Mahavidya cosmic form, whereas the Kali Stotram emphasises the more familiar four-armed cremation-ground Kali iconography. (Sources: comparison of Tantra Shastra and Brahmavaivarta Purana versions.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the Kali Stotram say about reciting it three times daily?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 14, the phalashruti, declares Idam stotram Maheshani Kalyah parama-durlabham, trisandhyam yah pathen-nityam tasya Lakshmih sthira bhavet — meaning O Maheshani, this stotra of Kali is supremely rare, and one who recites it daily at the three sandhyas (sunrise, noon and sunset) finds Lakshmi becoming stable in his life. This is the famous Sthira Lakshmi promise of the Kali Stotram, and it is unusual because Kali — typically associated with destruction and the cremation ground — is here said to bestow stable wealth. The traditional explanation is that Kali removes the alakshmi (the obstacles to prosperity) which lets stable Lakshmi take her seat. (Sources: Tantra Shastra phalashruti; Sir John Woodroffe Shakta commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Kali's iconography described in the stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 2 of the Kali Stotram describes Kali holding khadgam (sword) for cutting through delusion, kapalam (skull-cup) for collecting the nectar of self-knowledge after the death of the ego, damarum (drum) producing the cosmic sound of creation, and trishulam (trident) representing the three gunas she transcends. Her muktakeshi (loose disheveled hair) symbolises absolute freedom beyond all conventions, her mundamala (skull-garland) represents the fifty Sanskrit letters and the cycles of births and deaths, her digambari (sky-clad) form denotes that the absolute is beyond all coverings, and her position on Shiva (shava-vahana) shows shakti acting upon the substratum of pure consciousness. (Sources: Mahanirvana Tantra; Karpuradi Stotra commentary.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Kali Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kali Stotram is recited daily by Shakta devotees, ideally at the three sandhyas as the phalashruti recommends, but at minimum once daily before sunrise. It is given special importance on Tuesdays and Saturdays (sacred to fierce Devi forms), every amavasya night (especially Kartika Amavasya which is Kali Puja and Diwali), Chaitra and Sharada Navratris, the dark-half Ashtami of every month (Kalashtami), and during periods of personal crisis, fear, illness, hostile black magic, or grief. It is particularly recommended for those passing through difficult Saturn (Shani) or Rahu transits or undergoing tantric sadhana. (Sources: Drikpanchang Kali calendar; Tantra Shastra ritual manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: Is initiation required to recite the Kali Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The full tantric mantra-sadhana of Kali — including the bija Krim, the larger Dakshina Kali mantras, and the elaborate yantra worship — strictly requires diksha from a qualified Shakta guru. The stotra itself, however, being a devotional praise hymn, is accessible to all sincere devotees as an act of bhakti. Devotees should approach the recitation with reverence and a clean mind rather than treating it casually. The phalashruti specifies devotion (bhaktiman) and concentration (samahitah) as the qualifications, not formal initiation. For those drawn to deeper sadhana, finding an authentic guru in the Ramakrishna, Aurobindonian, or traditional tantric lineages is the recommended next step. (Sources: traditional Shakta guidance; Sri Ramakrishna Mission teachings.)
